workout.title: Chest Day - BODYBUILD
workout.description: Chest Day workout focusing on bodybuild training.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Retract and depress the shoulder blades (pinch the shoulder blades together) to create a stable chest platform.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over the chest so it is balanced and steady.
Inhale, lower the bar under control to the mid-chest (sternum) keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar touching or just above the chest while maintaining tightness through the core and legs.
Exhale and drive the bar upward by extending the elbows, pressing the bar back to the start position while keeping wrists neutral.
Avoid excessive elbow flare or bouncing the bar off the chest; maintain a controlled tempo and keep feet planted.
After the set, carefully re-rack the bar by guiding it back onto the bench supports before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie back so you are supine on the bench, feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and maintain a neutral spine with a slight natural arch; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a flat bench with a dumbbell resting on each thigh.
Lie back so you are supine on the bench, feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and maintain a neutral spine with a slight natural arch; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
Use your legs and core to help bring the dumbbells up to chest height so your upper arms are about 45 degrees from your torso and palms are facing forward (or slightly inward if preferred).

Inhale, then slowly lower the dumbbells in a controlled manner until your elbows are roughly level with the bench or just below chest level, keeping wrists neutral and elbows tracking slightly tucked.

Pause briefly at the bottom without relaxing the shoulder blade position, then exhale and press the dumbbells upward and slightly together until your arms are almost fully extended — avoid locking the elbows.
Control the descent back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
To finish the set, bring the dumbbells safely to your knees, sit up, and set them down or have a spotter assist if using heavy weight.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bench press is a supine pressing movement performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and delts for elbow and shoulder extension. Dumbbells allow a greater range of motion and require more stabilization from the forearms and serratus anterior.

exercises.name: Cable Bent Over Single Arm Crossover
exercises.notes: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to chest height and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to chest height and select an appropriate weight.

Stand to the side of the pulley with a staggered or shoulder-width stance and hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly 30–45° from vertical (slight bend in knees); maintain a neutral spine and brace your abs.
Grab the handle with the hand farthest from the machine, arm extended out to the side and palm facing down or slightly inward; allow a soft bend in the elbow.

Keep your shoulder down and stable, then exhale and pull the handle in a horizontal arc across your body toward the center of your chest, leading with the elbow and squeezing the pectorals at the end range.
Pause briefly with the chest contracted, keeping the torso still (avoid rotating the ribs or shoulders), then inhale and slowly reverse the motion to return the hand to the start under control.
Perform the desired reps, then switch sides and repeat. Maintain wrist neutrality and controlled tempo throughout.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable movement performed from a bent-over stance that sweeps the working arm across the body to load and compress the chest fibers, emphasizing the inner and upper pectoral region while the torso is stabilized.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

workout.title: Back Day - BODYBUILD
workout.description: Back Day workout focusing on bodybuild training.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Cable Cross over Lateral Pulldown
exercises.notes: Setup: Attach single D-handles to both high pulleys of a cable crossover or set a straight bar on the high pulley; select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Setup: Attach single D-handles to both high pulleys of a cable crossover or set a straight bar on the high pulley; select an appropriate weight.

Starting position: Stand between the towers or slightly in front of them with feet shoulder-width apart, knees soft, and a slight forward lean from the hips; grasp the handles with an overhand or neutral grip at shoulder height.
Brace your core and pull the shoulder blades down and back to initiate the movement.
Execution: Pull the handles down and slightly across your body by driving your elbows toward your sides and back, leading with the elbows rather than the hands.
Continue the pull until your hands are roughly at or just below chest level and you feel a full contraction in the lats and upper back; squeeze your shoulder blades together at the bottom.
Return: Slowly allow the cables to raise your hands back to the start position with controlled tension, keeping the torso stable and core engaged.
Coaching tips: Avoid excessive torso rotation or using momentum; keep a slight forward lean and focus on elbow-driven movement. Breathe out on the pull and inhale on the return.
exercises.description: Standing cable pulldown from the high crossover position, pulling the handles down and slightly across the body to load the latissimus dorsi and upper-back musculature. Performed from a slight forward-leaning stance while driving the elbows down and back and squeezing the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Cable High Row with Chest Support
exercises.notes: Set two cable pulleys to a high position and attach single-hand or D handles; choose a moderate weight.
instructions: Set two cable pulleys to a high position and attach single-hand or D handles; choose a moderate weight.

Position an incline bench or chest pad in front of the machine so you can sit facing the pulleys with your chest pressed firmly against the pad and feet flat on the floor; adjust pad height so torso is supported and arms can reach the handles overhead.
Sit tall with chest against the pad, neutral neck, core braced and shoulders down away from ears; reach up and grasp the handles with a neutral or overhand grip.
Begin with arms nearly extended overhead, scapulae slightly protracted; inhale to prepare.
Exhale and pull the handles down and back toward the upper chest by driving your elbows down and out, leading with the elbows and squeezing the shoulder blades together at the end range.
Hold the contracted position briefly (1–2 seconds) while maintaining a supported chest and no torso rotation.
Slowly release the handles under control, allowing the arms to extend back to the start while keeping tension on the cables and scapulae engaged.
Repeat for desired reps; avoid using momentum, leaning away from the pad, or shrugging the shoulders upward.
exercises.description: Seated chest-supported cable high row performed from dual high pulleys; you pull the handles down and back toward the upper chest while the chest rests against the pad to isolate the upper back. Emphasizes scapular retraction and posterior shoulder activation with reduced lower-back involvement.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

workout.title: Shoulder Day - BODYBUILD
workout.description: Shoulder Day workout focusing on bodybuild training.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Shoulders Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and knees slightly soft.
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and knees slightly soft.
Place the barbell in a front-rack position resting on the upper chest/clavicle area with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width and full grip around the bar.
Retract the shoulder blades slightly, keep the chest up and core braced (neutral spine); avoid excessive lumbar arching.
Take a full breath and begin the press by driving the bar upward in a straight line, slightly moving the head back as the bar passes and then returning the head forward under the bar.
Fully extend the elbows at the top with the bar directly over or slightly behind the head, shoulders stacked over hips and feet for balance.
Lower the bar under control back to the front-rack position to complete one rep while inhaling, keeping the elbows tracking slightly forward.
Repeat for desired repetitions, maintaining tight core engagement and controlled tempo; stop or reduce weight if you must excessively lean back to finish reps.
exercises.description: A standing barbell overhead press performed from the front-rack position that primarily develops the delts while requiring the triceps and upper back to assist and stabilize. The lifter presses the barbell from shoulder level to full arm extension overhead while maintaining a braced core.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Reverse Fly
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height so the handles are at chest/shoulder level and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the seat height so the handles are at chest/shoulder level and select an appropriate weight.
Sit upright with your chest pressed firmly against the chest pad, feet flat on the floor and a neutral spine; grip the handles with a neutral or overhand grip.
Start with your arms extended forward and a slight bend in the elbows, shoulders relaxed.
Exhale and pull the handles outward and back in a wide arc at shoulder height, leading with the elbows and squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Stop when your arms are roughly in line with your torso or you feel a strong contraction in the rear delts and upper back; keep movement controlled and avoid shrugging.
Inhale and slowly return the handles to the start position with control, maintaining the slight elbow bend and chest against the pad.
Repeat for the desired number of reps while keeping the torso stationary and avoiding momentum or swinging.
exercises.description: Seated reverse fly performed on a lever machine to strengthen the posterior delts and upper back by horizontally abducting the arms while seated with chest supported. The movement emphasizes scapular retraction and rear shoulder activation.

exercises.name: Cable Incline Y Raise
exercises.notes: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.
instructions: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.
Attach wrist straps to low pulley handles (both sides) and set a light–moderate weight so you can control the entire range.
Sit with feet flat on the floor, chest against the bench, and grasp the wrist straps so your arms hang down in front of you with a slight bend in the elbows; palms should face each other.
Retract and depress your shoulder blades slightly to stabilize the scapulae and maintain a neutral spine throughout the set.
Exhale and raise your arms up and out in a diagonal plane (about 30–45° forward of the frontal plane) to form a Y with your body; lead with the thumbs and keep elbows soft.
Lift until your arms are roughly in line with your head/ears (or you feel a solid contraction in the delts/traps), squeezing the upper back at the top.
Inhale and lower the arms slowly and under control back to the start position, resisting the cable’s pull.

Perform the desired reps while avoiding momentum, excessive lumbar extension, or letting the shoulders excessively shrug; keep the back supported by the bench and use wrist straps to minimize grip fatigue.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench with back support, you use low-set cables and wrist straps to lift the arms into a Y shape overhead, emphasizing the shoulder delts and the upper back/trap region. The movement is performed with a controlled lift and slow lowering to isolate the posterior/lateral delts and scapular stabilizers.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.notes: Light weight, focus on form and range of motion

workout.title: Arm Day - BODYBUILD
workout.description: Arm Day workout focusing on bodybuild training.

exercises.name: Barbell Lying Triceps Extension Skull Crusher
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor for stability and your head near the end of the bench so the bar can be lowered toward your forehead.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor for stability and your head near the end of the bench so the bar can be lowered toward your forehead.
Grip the barbell with a shoulder-width (or slightly narrower) overhand grip and press it up so your arms are fully extended above your chest; wrists should be neutral and stacked over elbows.
Keep your upper arms fixed and perpendicular to the floor (or slightly angled toward your head) throughout the set — only the forearm should move.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar by flexing the elbows, bringing the bar toward your forehead or just above your temples in a controlled arc.
Pause briefly when the bar is a few inches from your forehead (avoid actually hitting your head) and maintain tension in the triceps.
Exhale and extend the elbows to press the bar back up to the start position, locking out but avoiding hyperextension of the elbows.
Keep wrists straight and avoid flaring the elbows outward or letting the upper arms drift; use a spotter if loading heavy for safety.
Perform repetitions with slow, controlled tempo and stop the set if you feel shoulder or wrist pain — reduce weight if form breaks down.
exercises.description: A supine barbell isolation exercise that works elbow extension to load the triceps by lowering the bar toward the forehead (skull crusher) and extending back up. Emphasizes controlled movement from the elbow while keeping the upper arms relatively stationary.

exercises.name: Cable One Arm Curl
exercises.notes: Attach a single handle to the low pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Attach a single handle to the low pulley and select an appropriate weight.
Stand upright facing the machine with feet hip-width apart and a slight bend in the knees; grab the handle with a supinated (palm-up) grip using one hand.
Start with the working arm fully extended at your side and the elbow close to your torso; brace your core (abs) and maintain a neutral spine.
Keeping the upper arm stationary and the elbow fixed at your side, curl the handle upward by contracting the biceps until your forearm is near vertical or the knuckles approach shoulder height.
Pause briefly at the top while squeezing the biceps, then slowly lower the handle back to the start position under control to emphasize the eccentric phase.
Avoid swinging the torso or using the shoulder and delts to lift; if you need momentum, reduce the weight.
Perform the desired reps, then switch sides and repeat with the opposite arm, maintaining the same posture and tempo for both sides.
exercises.description: Standing single-arm curl using a low cable pulley to load the biceps through the concentric curl and controlled eccentric lowering; performed one arm at a time to emphasize unilateral strength and minimize momentum. Keep the elbow tucked to isolate the biceps and use a full range of motion.

exercises.name: Cable One Arm Triceps Pushdown
exercises.notes: Attach a single handle to the high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Attach a single handle to the high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
Stand facing the machine with feet hip- to shoulder-width apart and a slight bend in the knees; grab the handle with one hand in a neutral grip.
Tuck the working elbow to your side so the upper arm is stationary and bent about 90°; brace your core and adopt a slight forward lean from the hips if needed for balance.
Exhale and press the handle down by extending the elbow until the arm is fully straight and the triceps are contracted; keep the wrist neutral.
Pause briefly at full extension, focusing on squeezing the triceps and avoiding shoulder or upper-arm movement.
Inhale and slowly return the handle to the start position under control until the elbow is about 90°; do not let the upper arm drift away from your side.
Repeat for the desired reps, then switch to the other arm. Avoid using body momentum or swinging to move the weight.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable pushdown that isolates the triceps by extending the elbow against the cable's resistance while the upper arm remains stationary at the side. Performed standing with a single handle attached to a high pulley for constant tension.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Smith Front Squat (Clean Grip)
exercises.notes: Set the Smith machine bar at about shoulder height and load weight as needed; step inside the rack so the bar is in front of your shoulders.
instructions: Set the Smith machine bar at about shoulder height and load weight as needed; step inside the rack so the bar is in front of your shoulders.
Reach under the bar with a clean (front-rack) grip: fingers under the bar, elbows up and forward so the bar rests on the front of your shoulders/clavicles; hands about shoulder-width apart.
Unrack the bar by straightening your legs and take 1–2 steps back to position your feet roughly shoulder-width apart with toes pointing slightly outward; keep weight on midfoot.
Brace your core (abs) and keep chest up with elbows high to maintain an upright torso; this prevents the bar from rolling forward.

Begin the descent by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, lowering until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor or to your comfortable depth while keeping knees tracking over toes.
Drive up through your heels and midfoot, extending knees and hips simultaneously to return to the start, maintaining elbow position and a tall chest throughout the ascent.
Control each rep; avoid letting the knees collapse inward and keep movements smooth. When finished, step forward and carefully rack the bar back onto the machine.
exercises.description: A front-loaded squat performed in a Smith machine using the clean (front-rack) grip; it emphasizes upright torso position and places primary load on the quadriceps while also engaging the posterior chain and core. The fixed bar path of the Smith machine helps control the vertical movement.

exercises.name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Brace your core, pull your shoulders back slightly, and set a neutral spine; keep a small, fixed bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not squat.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over the middle of your feet; grip the bar just outside your thighs with an overhand or mixed grip.
Brace your core, pull your shoulders back slightly, and set a neutral spine; keep a small, fixed bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not squat.
Initiate the movement by pushing your hips straight back (hip hinge) and allow the bar to descend close to your legs, keeping your chest up and eyes forward.
Continue hinging until you feel a stretch in the hamstrings (bar typically reaches just below the knees or mid-shin depending on flexibility) while maintaining a flat back.
Pause briefly at the bottom without rounding the lower back; keep the lats engaged to keep the bar close to the body.
Drive the hips forward by contracting the glutes and hamstrings to return to a standing position, finishing with hips fully extended but without hyperextending the spine.
Breathe: inhale on the descent, exhale as you drive the hips forward. Repeat for desired repetitions, focusing on controlled tempo and maintaining the hip-hinge pattern.
exercises.description: A hip-hinge movement performed standing with a barbell that emphasizes eccentric and concentric loading of the hamstrings and glutes while the posterior chain and grip stabilize the load. Maintain a neutral spine and a soft knee angle throughout the movement.

exercises.name: Smith Standing Leg Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Set the Smith bar to a comfortable shoulder height and place a stable raised platform or block under your feet so your heels can drop below the platform.
instructions: Set the Smith bar to a comfortable shoulder height and place a stable raised platform or block under your feet so your heels can drop below the platform.

Step under the bar and position it across your upper back/trapezius (or hold it at hip level if demonstrated), then unlock the Smith carriage and stand with feet hip-width apart and toes pointing forward on the platform edge.
Keep knees straight or with a very slight bend, chest up, and core engaged for a stable upright posture.
Exhale and press through the balls of both feet to raise your heels as high as possible, fully plantarflexing the ankles and squeezing the calves at the top.
Hold the top position briefly, then inhale and slowly lower your heels down below the platform level to achieve a full stretch in the calves.
Control the movement; avoid bouncing or using trunk momentum. Repeat for the desired repetitions, then carefully re-rack the Smith bar when finished.
exercises.description: A standing calf raise performed on a Smith machine that increases resistance for plantarflexion to develop the gastrocnemius and soleus. The lifter rises onto the balls of the feet from a raised surface then lowers the heels below the platform for a full stretch.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.
Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.
Avoid knee collapse (valgus) — consciously push knees outward and keep toes engaged. Do not let the lower back round; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you ascend and extending hips and knees simultaneously until fully upright.
Reset your breathing and bracing between reps as needed. When finished, step forward under control and re-rack the bar securely.
Use appropriate weight, a spotter or safety pins in the rack if lifting near maximum loads, and adjust stance or bar position to reduce discomfort in shoulders or lower back.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip, feet about hip‑width apart and toes pointing forward.
instructions: Stand upright holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip, feet about hip‑width apart and toes pointing forward.
Set a slight bend in the knees (soft knees) and brace your core to stabilize the spine before initiating the movement.

Hinge at the hips by pushing your hips straight back while keeping the spine neutral and chest slightly lifted; allow the dumbbells to slide down the front of your thighs and shins, staying close to the body.
Lower until you feel a strong stretch in the hamstrings or the dumbbells reach mid‑shin (maintain neutral cervical alignment, do not round the lower back).
Reverse the motion by driving the hips forward, squeezing the glutes and hamstrings to return to standing while keeping the knees softly bent and the weights close to the legs.
Maintain controlled tempo (slow on the descent, controlled drive on the ascent), keep the shoulders down away from the ears, and avoid using the lower back to lift.
exercises.description: A standing hip‑hinge exercise performed with dumbbells that emphasizes eccentric loading of the hamstrings while the hips move back and forth; the movement also engages the glutes and core to maintain a neutral spine. Keep a slight knee bend and the weights close to the legs throughout.

exercises.name: Cable Donkey Diagonal Kickback
exercises.notes: Set the cable pulley low and attach an ankle cuff to the working ankle; select a moderate weight.
instructions: Set the cable pulley low and attach an ankle cuff to the working ankle; select a moderate weight.
Stand facing the cable column and hinge slightly at the hips so your torso is bent forward; brace your hands on the frame or a stable support at chest height for balance (standing, braced position).
Shift your weight onto the supporting leg and keep that knee slightly bent; the working leg should be unloaded and ready to move.

Keep your torso stable and core engaged (avoid excessive lumbar extension or rotation) and, with a slight bend in the working knee, drive the cuffed ankle back and upward in a diagonal arc (outward and back) until the glute is fully contracted.
Pause and squeeze the glute at the top for 1–2 seconds while maintaining a neutral pelvis and stable upper body.
Slowly lower the leg back to the start position under control, keeping tension on the cable and avoiding the leg simply dropping.
Perform the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs and repeat on the other side.
exercises.description: A cable variation of the donkey kick that drives the working leg backward and slightly outward on a diagonal to emphasize the glute muscles while the cable provides constant resistance. Performed standing with an ankle cuff and torso braced on the cable frame to isolate the glutes.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

workout.title: Upper Body - STRENGTH
workout.description: Upper Body workout focusing on strength training.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.
instructions: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.

Lie supine on the bench with your head, upper back and glutes in contact with the bench, feet flat on the floor and a small natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists should be stacked over the elbows and forearms vertical when the bar is over the chest.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar out over the chest while keeping the scapulae stable; lock the elbows briefly before starting the set.
Inhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest/sternum (around nipple level), keeping the elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso to protect the shoulders.
Allow the bar to touch or come very close to the chest without bouncing; maintain tension in the pectorals and keep the feet and glutes braced.
Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders stable, then begin the next rep. Maintain consistent form and breathing throughout the set.
Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack for heavy sets, and avoid flaring the elbows excessively or letting the lower back lift off the bench.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

exercises.name: Barbell Clean and Press
exercises.notes: Start standing with feet about hip-width to slightly wider, barbell over mid-foot; grip the bar just outside the knees with a double overhand or hook grip and chest up.
instructions: Start standing with feet about hip-width to slightly wider, barbell over mid-foot; grip the bar just outside the knees with a double overhand or hook grip and chest up.
Hinge at the hips and bend the knees so your hips are low, back flat, shoulders slightly in front of the bar and eyes forward (front of a squat rack/sightline).
Initiate the lift by driving through the heels and extending the hips and knees explosively while keeping the bar close to your body; shrug forcefully at full extension.

As you shrug, pull the elbows high and outside to accelerate the bar upward and quickly drop under the bar into a partial front-rack squat to 'catch' the bar on your shoulders (front rack position) with elbows high.
Stand up tall from the catch, fully extending the hips and knees to a stable upright front-rack position with the bar resting on the shoulders/clavicles and elbows forward.

From the front-rack, press the bar overhead by driving through the legs slightly (if using a push press) or strictly extend the arms (strict press), finishing with arms locked overhead and head slightly forward under the bar.

Lower the bar back to the front-rack in a controlled manner, then return the bar to the floor by hinging at the hips and bending the knees (reset) or carefully drop into a controlled descent if using appropriate technique and environment.
Maintain a braced core and neutral spine throughout, keep the bar path close to the body, and breathe/exhale during the press portion; reset between reps if necessary to preserve form.
exercises.description: A compound Olympic-style lift that combines a barbell clean (explosive hip extension and pull to bring the bar to the front rack) with a strict or push overhead press to finish. Trains shoulder pressing strength while recruiting legs, posterior chain, upper back and core for power and stability.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Prioritize form over weight - quality reps build strength.

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.
Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.
Avoid knee collapse (valgus) — consciously push knees outward and keep toes engaged. Do not let the lower back round; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you ascend and extending hips and knees simultaneously until fully upright.
Reset your breathing and bracing between reps as needed. When finished, step forward under control and re-rack the bar securely.
Use appropriate weight, a spotter or safety pins in the rack if lifting near maximum loads, and adjust stance or bar position to reduce discomfort in shoulders or lower back.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Zercher Back Extension
exercises.notes: Set up on a hyperextension / Roman chair: anchor your feet, position the hip pad under the front of your hips/upper thighs so your torso can hinge freely, and face down (prone).
instructions: Set up on a hyperextension / Roman chair: anchor your feet, position the hip pad under the front of your hips/upper thighs so your torso can hinge freely, and face down (prone).
Cradle a barbell in the crook of your elbows (Zercher hold) and keep the bar close to your body; cross your forearms or grip the bar to secure it and keep elbows tucked.
Start with your torso lowered toward the floor until you feel a stretch in the hamstrings and glutes or your body is roughly perpendicular to the pad (hip flexion). Keep a neutral neck and spine.

Drive your hips forward and contract your glutes and hamstrings to raise your torso until your body forms a straight line from head to heels or you reach a neutral spine — do not hyperextend the lower back.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tight core and glute contraction, then slowly lower back to the start position under control.
Breathe out as you extend up, inhale as you lower. Maintain tension in the hips and avoid bending at the knees or using momentum.
exercises.description: A hip-extension back extension performed on a Roman chair while holding a barbell in the Zercher (cradle of the elbows) position; it emphasizes hip drive to raise the torso and targets the glutes and hamstrings while the erector spinae stabilizes the spine.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Prioritize form over weight - quality reps build strength.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.
instructions: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.

Lie supine on the bench with your head, upper back and glutes in contact with the bench, feet flat on the floor and a small natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists should be stacked over the elbows and forearms vertical when the bar is over the chest.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar out over the chest while keeping the scapulae stable; lock the elbows briefly before starting the set.
Inhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest/sternum (around nipple level), keeping the elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso to protect the shoulders.
Allow the bar to touch or come very close to the chest without bouncing; maintain tension in the pectorals and keep the feet and glutes braced.
Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders stable, then begin the next rep. Maintain consistent form and breathing throughout the set.
Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack for heavy sets, and avoid flaring the elbows excessively or letting the lower back lift off the bench.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Press Squat
exercises.notes: Start standing with feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward and a dumbbell held vertically at chest in a goblet grip (hands around the head/handle), elbows close to your body.
instructions: Start standing with feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward and a dumbbell held vertically at chest in a goblet grip (hands around the head/handle), elbows close to your body.
Brace your core, pull your shoulders down and back, keep chest up and eyes forward throughout the movement.
Inhale and hinge at the hips while bending the knees to lower into a squat — push hips back and let knees track over toes until thighs are at least parallel to the floor.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you rise and maintaining a strong upright torso.
Once standing, press the dumbbell overhead (or slightly forward depending on comfort), extending the elbows and engaging delts/pectorals; exhale during the press.
Slowly lower the dumbbell back to the goblet position at your chest with control, resetting your brace before the next descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, keeping movements steady, knees tracking toes and avoiding rounded lower back or knees collapsing inward.
exercises.description: A standing compound movement that combines a goblet-style squat with a dumbbell press; it primarily works the quads while also engaging the chest, shoulders and arms. Perform by squatting from a goblet hold and then pressing the weight as you stand.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Prioritize form over weight - quality reps build strength.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Palm-in Press
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with feet flat on the floor. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with feet flat on the floor.
Pick up a dumbbell in each hand and sit upright on the bench, then lie back so your head, upper back, and glutes are supported (supine on an incline).
Hold the dumbbells at shoulder level with a neutral grip (palms facing each other) and elbows slightly tucked (~30–45 degrees from the torso).
Brace your core, retract your shoulder blades, and keep your feet planted.
Exhale and press the dumbbells upward and slightly toward each other until your arms are nearly extended but not fully locked.
Pause briefly at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position with controlled tension on the pectorals.
Keep wrists neutral (don’t let them bend), maintain the incline bench contact, and avoid flaring the elbows straight out to minimize shoulder strain.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions with steady, controlled tempo.
exercises.description: An incline dumbbell press performed with a neutral (palms-in) grip that emphasizes the upper portion of the chest while reducing stress on the shoulders. Performed on an incline bench, pressing the weights up and slightly together from chest level.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Wide Lat Pulldown
exercises.notes: Step 1: Sit on the machine seat and adjust the thigh pads so your legs are secured and you cannot be lifted by the bar. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Step 1: Sit on the machine seat and adjust the thigh pads so your legs are secured and you cannot be lifted by the bar.
Step 2: Select an appropriate weight and reach up with a wide, pronated (palms facing forward) grip on the lever bar outside shoulder width.
Step 3: Sit upright with a slight natural arch in the lower back, chest lifted and a very slight lean back from the hips; keep feet flat on the floor.
Step 4: Brace your core, exhale, and initiate the movement by retracting the shoulder blades and driving your elbows down and back to pull the bar toward the upper chest.
Step 5: Pause briefly at the bottom and squeeze the lats and upper back without shrugging the shoulders excessively.
Step 6: Inhale and slowly return the bar to the start position with controlled motion until the arms are nearly fully extended while maintaining tension in the lats.
Step 7: Repeat for the desired reps; avoid using momentum or excessive torso lean — keep movement controlled and driven by the back and elbow action.
exercises.description: A seated machine pulldown using a wide grip to emphasize the latissimus dorsi and upper back musculature; performed from a seated position with thigh pads to stabilize the torso. The movement pulls the bar down toward the upper chest while driving the elbows down and back.

exercises.name: Cable Incline Y Raise
exercises.notes: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.
Attach wrist straps to low pulley handles (both sides) and set a light–moderate weight so you can control the entire range.
Sit with feet flat on the floor, chest against the bench, and grasp the wrist straps so your arms hang down in front of you with a slight bend in the elbows; palms should face each other.
Retract and depress your shoulder blades slightly to stabilize the scapulae and maintain a neutral spine throughout the set.
Exhale and raise your arms up and out in a diagonal plane (about 30–45° forward of the frontal plane) to form a Y with your body; lead with the thumbs and keep elbows soft.
Lift until your arms are roughly in line with your head/ears (or you feel a solid contraction in the delts/traps), squeezing the upper back at the top.
Inhale and lower the arms slowly and under control back to the start position, resisting the cable’s pull.

Perform the desired reps while avoiding momentum, excessive lumbar extension, or letting the shoulders excessively shrug; keep the back supported by the bench and use wrist straps to minimize grip fatigue.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench with back support, you use low-set cables and wrist straps to lift the arms into a Y shape overhead, emphasizing the shoulder delts and the upper back/trap region. The movement is performed with a controlled lift and slow lowering to isolate the posterior/lateral delts and scapular stabilizers.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Allow full recovery between exercises - this is not cardio.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Decline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set a decline bench to a comfortable angle and hook your feet under the foot pads so your body is stable; lie supine with your head lower than your hips. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set a decline bench to a comfortable angle and hook your feet under the foot pads so your body is stable; lie supine with your head lower than your hips.
Pick up a dumbbell in each hand and hold them above your chest with a pronated (palms facing forward) grip and arms extended but not locked.
Retract your shoulder blades slightly and brace your core to maintain a stable torso throughout the movement.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells in a controlled arc to the lower chest/ribcage area, keeping your elbows at about a 30–45° angle from your torso.
Pause briefly when the dumbbells reach chest level (do not bounce); maintain control and a steady posture with feet anchored.
Exhale and press the dumbbells upward until your arms are nearly straight without locking the elbows, squeezing the pectorals at the top.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then carefully return the dumbbells to your thighs and sit up to place them down to exit the bench safely.
Coaching cues: avoid excessive lower back arch, keep movements controlled, and do not let the elbows flare directly out at 90° to reduce shoulder strain.
exercises.description: Lying on a decline bench with feet secured, press a pair of dumbbells upward to emphasize the lower portion of the pectoral muscles. The movement is a pressing exercise performed with a pronated grip and controlled lowering to the chest.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Single Arm Row
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat (and chest pad if available) so the handle is reachable with your arm fully extended; select an appropriate weight and place feet flat on the foot platform. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the seat (and chest pad if available) so the handle is reachable with your arm fully extended; select an appropriate weight and place feet flat on the foot platform.
Sit upright with a neutral spine, chest lightly against the pad or upright on the seat, feet planted, and grasp the single handle with a neutral or overhand grip.
Begin with the working arm fully extended and the shoulder relaxed; brace your core and prevent torso rotation.
Exhale and pull the handle by driving the elbow straight back toward your hip, leading with the elbow and actively retracting the shoulder blade.
Pause 1–2 seconds at the top and squeeze the lats and upper back while keeping the torso still.
Inhale and slowly control the handle back to the start, fully extending the arm without collapsing the shoulder.
Repeat for the desired reps, then switch sides; maintain a controlled tempo and avoid using momentum or twisting the torso.
exercises.description: A unilateral seated row performed on a lever machine that emphasizes the lats by pulling a single handle back while keeping the torso stable. Each rep focuses on driving the elbow toward the hip and retracting the shoulder blade for a strong concentric contraction.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Raise
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with arms fully extended down at your sides and palms facing inward; keep a slight bend in the elbows.
Brace your core, keep your chest up and neck neutral, and avoid arching your lower back.
Exhale and lift both dumbbells upward and slightly forward in a controlled arc until your hands reach about shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor).
Pause briefly at the top, maintaining tension in the delts and avoiding momentum or swinging.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting hanging position under control.
Perform the desired repetitions, keeping movement smooth and avoiding use of torso momentum; reduce weight if you need to swing to complete reps.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench, this variation of the dumbbell raise emphasizes the front portion of the shoulder (anterior delts) by lifting the weights from a hanging position up to about shoulder height. The incline angle shifts some load onto the chest (pectorals) while isolating the delts and minimizing torso swing.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Allow full recovery between exercises - this is not cardio.

exercises.name: Lever Deadlift (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Approach the machine and hinge at the hips while maintaining a neutral spine; bend knees until you can grasp the handles with a firm overhand or mixed grip. Stop 1 rep short of failure (RPE 9). Spinal safety takes priority.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width to slightly wider, toes pointing forward or slightly outward, with the lever bar/handles positioned over mid-foot.
Approach the machine and hinge at the hips while maintaining a neutral spine; bend knees until you can grasp the handles with a firm overhand or mixed grip.
Set your chest up, pull shoulder blades slightly back, keep gaze forward and core braced; shoulders should be roughly over or slightly in front of the handles.
Drive through your heels and extend your hips and knees simultaneously to lift the lever, focusing on squeezing the glutes at full standing position; keep the spine neutral throughout.
Pause briefly at the top with hips fully extended (no hyperextension of the lower back), then control the descent by pushing hips back and bending knees to lower the lever back to the start.
Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining steady breathing (exhale on the drive up, inhale on the lowering) and avoid rounding the lower back or jerking the weight.
Adjust foot position, grip width, and load as needed; ensure machine safety stops or latches are set before beginning heavier sets.
exercises.description: Plate-loaded lever deadlift performed from a standing hip-hinge position; focuses on driving the hips forward to extend the legs and lift the lever, targeting the glutes and posterior chain. The movement uses a fixed lever arm and handles rather than a free barbell.

exercises.name: Barbell Floor Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Load a barbell and position it across the upper back/trapezius, not the neck; grip the bar just outside shoulder width with a firm hold. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Load a barbell and position it across the upper back/trapezius, not the neck; grip the bar just outside shoulder width with a firm hold.
Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and weight evenly distributed on the mid-foot; keep a slight bend in the knees (not locked).
Brace your core and maintain an upright torso; focus on keeping shoulders down and the bar stable on your traps/delts.
Slowly raise both heels by pushing through the balls of your feet until you reach full plantarflexion (stand on your toes); contract the calves at the top and hold for 1–2 seconds.
Lower your heels back to the floor in a controlled manner until the soles are flat, feeling a stretch in the calves.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, breathing out on the ascent and inhaling on the descent.
If balance is an issue, perform the movement near a wall or use a spotter; avoid bouncing or using excessive forward trunk lean to lift the weight.
exercises.description: Standing barbell calf raise performed with a barbell resting on the upper back to load the lower legs; raise the heels by plantarflexing the ankles, pause at the top, then lower with control. This exercise primarily isolates the gastrocnemius/soleus (calves) while the upper back and shoulders stabilize the load.

exercises.name: Dumbbell One Arm Row (rack support)
exercises.notes: Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor while keeping a slight bend in both knees; maintain a neutral spine and long neck throughout.
instructions: Stand with a staggered stance (one foot forward, one foot back) beside a rack; place the hand nearest the rack on the rail at about hip height for support.
Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor while keeping a slight bend in both knees; maintain a neutral spine and long neck throughout.
Let the working arm hang straight down holding a dumbbell with a neutral or overhand grip, shoulder relaxed.
Brace your core and pull the dumbbell upward in a vertical path, leading with the elbow and driving it toward your ribcage/hip; keep the elbow close to your body.
Squeeze the shoulder blade toward the spine at the top of the movement and pause briefly to maximize lat/upper-back contraction.
Slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position under control until the arm is fully extended, maintaining torso stability and avoiding rotation.
Complete the desired reps on one side, then switch sides so the opposite hand supports on the rack and repeat. Keep breathing steadily (exhale on the pull, inhale on the return).
exercises.description: A single-arm bent-over row performed with one hand bracing on a rack for support; the exercise emphasizes a unilateral pull to develop the lats while also engaging the upper back and arm muscles. The support hand and staggered stance increase stability and allow focus on a full, controlled contraction of the pulling side.

exercises.name: Reverse grip Pull-up
exercises.notes: Set up: grasp a pull-up bar with a supinated (underhand) grip, hands about shoulder-width apart or slightly narrower.
instructions: Set up: grasp a pull-up bar with a supinated (underhand) grip, hands about shoulder-width apart or slightly narrower.
Start position: hang fully extended with feet off the ground (legs can be crossed at the ankles or slightly bent) and scapula slightly depressed and retracted to remove slack.
Initiate the pull by driving the elbows down and back, engaging the lats and upper back rather than just curling with the arms.
Continue pulling until your chin passes the bar or your upper chest reaches the bar, keeping the torso as upright and controlled as possible.
Pause briefly at the top while squeezing the lats and upper back, avoiding excessive neck or shoulder shrugging.
Lower slowly and under control back to a full dead hang (arms fully extended) while maintaining scapular control.
Repeat for the desired number of reps. Breathe out on the pull and inhale as you lower. Avoid kipping or excessive swinging to keep tension on the target muscles.
exercises.description: A supinated (underhand) grip pull-up that emphasizes the latissimus and recruits the biceps more than a pronated pull-up. From a dead hang you pull your torso up until your chin or chest reaches the bar, then lower with control.

exercises.name: Chest Dip
exercises.notes: Start standing between parallel bars or on the dip station and grasp the handles with a neutral grip; press down to lift your body so your arms are straight and feet are off the ground (legs can be bent or crossed behind you).
instructions: Start standing between parallel bars or on the dip station and grasp the handles with a neutral grip; press down to lift your body so your arms are straight and feet are off the ground (legs can be bent or crossed behind you).
Lean your torso forward about 20–30 degrees from vertical to shift emphasis onto the pectorals while keeping the core braced and scapulae slightly depressed.
Begin the descent by inhaling and bending the elbows, allowing them to track slightly outward (not flared excessively) as you lower your chest toward the level of the bars.
Lower until your upper arms are about parallel to the floor or your chest is just below the bar level, maintaining control and a forward lean throughout.
Exhale and press through your palms, extending the elbows to return to the start position with straight arms; avoid locking the elbows at the top.
Perform repetitions with a slow, controlled tempo; use an assistance machine or band, or reduce range of motion if you cannot maintain proper form.
exercises.description: A bodyweight dip performed on parallel bars with a forward lean to emphasize the chest (pectorals). The movement lowers the torso by bending the elbows and then presses the body back up to the start position.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Press Squat
exercises.notes: Start standing with feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward and a dumbbell held vertically at chest in a goblet grip (hands around the head/handle), elbows close to your body.
instructions: Start standing with feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward and a dumbbell held vertically at chest in a goblet grip (hands around the head/handle), elbows close to your body.
Brace your core, pull your shoulders down and back, keep chest up and eyes forward throughout the movement.

Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you rise and maintaining a strong upright torso.
Once standing, press the dumbbell overhead (or slightly forward depending on comfort), extending the elbows and engaging delts/pectorals; exhale during the press.
Slowly lower the dumbbell back to the goblet position at your chest with control, resetting your brace before the next descent.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Raise
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with arms fully extended down at your sides and palms facing inward; keep a slight bend in the elbows.
Brace your core, keep your chest up and neck neutral, and avoid arching your lower back.
Exhale and lift both dumbbells upward and slightly forward in a controlled arc until your hands reach about shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor).
Pause briefly at the top, maintaining tension in the delts and avoiding momentum or swinging.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting hanging position under control.
Perform the desired repetitions, keeping movement smooth and avoiding use of torso momentum; reduce weight if you need to swing to complete reps.

The incline angle shifts some load onto the chest (pectorals) while isolating the delts and minimizing torso swing.

tips: Maintain a steady rhythm throughout each set.
Focus on breathing patterns - exhale on exertion.
Keep transitions between exercises quick.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Overhead Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees slightly soft (not locked).
instructions: Stand tall with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees slightly soft (not locked).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward or slightly neutral and elbows roughly under the wrists.
Brace your core and squeeze your glutes to maintain a neutral spine and prevent lower-back arching.
Exhale and press both dumbbells straight overhead in a controlled path until your arms are nearly fully extended (avoid jamming the elbows).
Keep the wrists stacked over the elbows and the chin tucked slightly; do not overextend the neck or lower back at the top.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts and scapular stabilizers.
Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly and under control back to the starting shoulder-height position.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining core tension and full control on both ascent and descent.

exercises.name: Cable Standing Row
exercises.notes: Set the cable pulley to about chest height and attach a close-grip or double-handle.
instructions: Set the cable pulley to about chest height and attach a close-grip or double-handle.
Stand facing the machine with feet hip-width apart (or a slight staggered stance for balance) and a soft bend in the knees; grasp the handle with a neutral or overhand grip.
Step back until the cable is taut and hinge slightly at the hips so your torso is upright but tilted forward a few degrees; brace your core and keep a neutral spine.
Begin the movement by pulling your shoulder blades together (scapular retraction), then drive your elbows straight back toward your sides, keeping them close to the body.
Pull until your hands reach your lower chest/upper abdomen and you feel a full contraction in the lats and upper back; avoid excessive torso lean or momentum.
Pause 0–1 seconds at the top while squeezing the shoulder blades, then slowly extend your arms back to the start under control, maintaining core tension.
Repeat for the desired reps and adjust weight so you can maintain strict form for the full set.
exercises.description: A standing cable rowing movement that targets the lats and mid/upper back by pulling a cable handle toward the torso while maintaining a braced, upright stance. Emphasizes scapular retraction and controlled concentric and eccentric phases.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Alternate Press
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and choose a pair of dumbbells you can control for the desired reps.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and choose a pair of dumbbells you can control for the desired reps.
Sit on the bench and plant your feet flat on the floor; lie back so your head, upper back and hips are supported (supine on an incline).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder level with elbows bent (~90°), wrists stacked over the elbows and palms facing forward or slightly neutral.
Brace your core and maintain a slight natural arch in the lower back; keep shoulder blades drawn lightly together and down.
Press one dumbbell upward and slightly toward the midline until the arm is nearly extended, exhaling on the press; avoid locking the elbow.
Lower that dumbbell slowly back to the start position while the opposite arm remains paused in the lowered/shoulder-height position.
Alternate and press the other dumbbell upward in the same controlled manner, inhaling as you lower each rep.
Continue alternating reps for the set, keeping movement controlled, wrists aligned, and avoiding excessive flaring of the elbows or letting the weights drop behind the head.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench, this exercise presses one dumbbell at a time upward in an alternating pattern to emphasize the upper portion of the chest while improving unilateral strength and stability.

exercises.name: Cable Bent Over Single Arm Crossover
exercises.notes: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to chest height and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to chest height and select an appropriate weight.

Stand to the side of the pulley with a staggered or shoulder-width stance and hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly 30–45° from vertical (slight bend in knees); maintain a neutral spine and brace your abs.
Grab the handle with the hand farthest from the machine, arm extended out to the side and palm facing down or slightly inward; allow a soft bend in the elbow.

Keep your shoulder down and stable, then exhale and pull the handle in a horizontal arc across your body toward the center of your chest, leading with the elbow and squeezing the pectorals at the end range.
Pause briefly with the chest contracted, keeping the torso still (avoid rotating the ribs or shoulders), then inhale and slowly reverse the motion to return the hand to the start under control.
Perform the desired reps, then switch sides and repeat. Maintain wrist neutrality and controlled tempo throughout.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable movement performed from a bent-over stance that sweeps the working arm across the body to load and compress the chest fibers, emphasizing the inner and upper pectoral region while the torso is stabilized.

tips: Maintain a steady rhythm throughout each set.
Focus on breathing patterns - exhale on exertion.
Keep transitions between exercises quick.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Retract and depress the shoulder blades (pinch the shoulder blades together) to create a stable chest platform.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over the chest so it is balanced and steady.
Inhale, lower the bar under control to the mid-chest (sternum) keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar touching or just above the chest while maintaining tightness through the core and legs.
Exhale and drive the bar upward by extending the elbows, pressing the bar back to the start position while keeping wrists neutral.
Avoid excessive elbow flare or bouncing the bar off the chest; maintain a controlled tempo and keep feet planted.
After the set, carefully re-rack the bar by guiding it back onto the bench supports before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Lever Incline Fly
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine so the back pad is set to an incline (approximately 30–45°) and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the machine so the back pad is set to an incline (approximately 30–45°) and select an appropriate weight.

Slightly bend your elbows and retract/depress your shoulder blades to stabilize the shoulders before starting the movement.
Exhale and drive the handles upward and inward in a controlled, wide arc, leading with the elbows while maintaining the slight elbow bend.
Bring the handles together above the upper chest and hold a brief squeeze (about 1 second) focusing on contracting the pectorals.

Inhale and slowly reverse the motion, resisting the weight as you lower the handles back to the starting position until you feel a controlled stretch in the chest—do not let the shoulders shrug or the elbows fully lock.
Repeat for the prescribed number of repetitions, keeping tempo controlled and avoiding momentum or excessive range that causes shoulder discomfort.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Wide Lat Pulldown
exercises.notes: Step 1: Sit on the machine seat and adjust the thigh pads so your legs are secured and you cannot be lifted by the bar.
instructions: Step 1: Sit on the machine seat and adjust the thigh pads so your legs are secured and you cannot be lifted by the bar.
Step 2: Select an appropriate weight and reach up with a wide, pronated (palms facing forward) grip on the lever bar outside shoulder width.
Step 3: Sit upright with a slight natural arch in the lower back, chest lifted and a very slight lean back from the hips; keep feet flat on the floor.
Step 4: Brace your core, exhale, and initiate the movement by retracting the shoulder blades and driving your elbows down and back to pull the bar toward the upper chest.
Step 5: Pause briefly at the bottom and squeeze the lats and upper back without shrugging the shoulders excessively.
Step 6: Inhale and slowly return the bar to the start position with controlled motion until the arms are nearly fully extended while maintaining tension in the lats.
Step 7: Repeat for the desired reps; avoid using momentum or excessive torso lean — keep movement controlled and driven by the back and elbow action.
exercises.description: A seated machine pulldown using a wide grip to emphasize the latissimus dorsi and upper back musculature; performed from a seated position with thigh pads to stabilize the torso. The movement pulls the bar down toward the upper chest while driving the elbows down and back.

exercises.name: Pull-up (neutral grip)
exercises.notes: Stand under the pull-up handles and reach up to grasp the parallel (neutral) grips with palms facing each other, hands about shoulder-width apart.
instructions: Stand under the pull-up handles and reach up to grasp the parallel (neutral) grips with palms facing each other, hands about shoulder-width apart.
Hang fully with arms extended, shoulders relaxed but slightly depressed and scapulae engaged (avoid hanging completely passive); cross or bend your legs to reduce swinging if needed.
Brace your core and initiate the movement by pulling your shoulder blades down and together, then drive your elbows down toward your ribs while pulling your chest up toward the handles.
Continue pulling until your chin clears the level of your hands or your chest reaches the handles, keeping the torso relatively upright and avoiding excessive kipping.
Pause briefly at the top with shoulders engaged, then lower yourself slowly and under control back to the full-hang position with arms extended.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining smooth controlled reps and avoiding momentum or excessive body swing.
exercises.description: A neutral-grip pull-up performed hanging from parallel handles with palms facing each other; you pull the torso upward until the chin clears the hands, emphasizing vertical pulling with strong lat engagement. This variation increases biceps involvement and is slightly easier on the shoulders than a wide pronated grip.

exercises.name: Chin-Up
exercises.notes: Grasp a pull-up bar with a supinated (underhand) grip, hands about shoulder-width apart; hang from the bar with arms fully extended and feet off the ground (dead hang).
instructions: Grasp a pull-up bar with a supinated (underhand) grip, hands about shoulder-width apart; hang from the bar with arms fully extended and feet off the ground (dead hang).
Brace your core and retract your shoulder blades slightly to stabilize the torso before initiating the pull.
Drive your elbows down and back by contracting your biceps and lats, pulling your chest toward the bar.
Continue pulling until your chin clears the bar or the bar touches your upper chest, keeping the torso relatively upright and avoiding excessive swinging.
Pause briefly at the top and squeeze the working muscles (biceps and upper back).
Lower yourself slowly and with control back to the full hang, extending the arms fully while maintaining shoulder blade stability.
Breathe out during the ascent and inhale during the controlled descent.
If you cannot complete a full chin-up, use a band-assisted variation, a box/step for negative-only reps, or an assisted chin-up machine to reduce load while maintaining the same body position.
exercises.description: A vertical pulling exercise performed from a hanging position with a supinated (underhand) grip that primarily loads the biceps while also working the lats and upper back. Pull your chin over the bar and lower under control to build upper-body pulling strength.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.notes: Stand upright (standing) with feet about hip-width apart and knees soft; hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward or neutral and elbows bent. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Stand upright (standing) with feet about hip-width apart and knees soft; hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward or neutral and elbows bent.
Brace your abs and keep your chest up to maintain a neutral spine; avoid overarching the lower back.
Press one dumbbell overhead by extending the elbow and driving through the shoulder until the arm is nearly or fully straight (do not violently lock the elbow).
Pause briefly at the top, keeping the shoulder engaged and scapula stable (avoid shrugging excessively).
Lower that dumbbell under control back to shoulder height while maintaining core tension.
Immediately press the opposite dumbbell overhead, alternating arms with each repetition in a steady, controlled rhythm.
Breathe out during each press and inhale as you lower; choose a weight that allows full control and good form for the desired rep range.

exercises.name: Cable Standing Lat Pulldown
exercises.notes: Set a high pulley with a straight or slightly curved lat bar (or rope) attached at the top of the machine and select an appropriate weight. Train to muscular failure.

Reach up and grasp the bar with an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width; maintain a neutral spine and braced core (abs engaged).

Pull the bar down in a controlled path toward the upper chest or collarbone area, bending the elbows and driving the elbows down and back; squeeze the lats at the bottom of the movement.

Slowly return the bar to the start position with controlled tension until arms are nearly extended but shoulders remain engaged; do not let the weight stack slam.

exercises.name: Sumo Deadlift High Pull
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips and bend the knees to lower into a deep squat (sumo position) until your hips are below or near knee level; keep chest up and maintain a neutral spine.
instructions: Stand with a wide (sumo) stance: feet wider than shoulder-width, toes turned slightly outward and bar over the midfoot.
Hinge at the hips and bend the knees to lower into a deep squat (sumo position) until your hips are below or near knee level; keep chest up and maintain a neutral spine.
Grip the bar with both hands inside your knees (narrow grip) and engage your core; shoulders should be slightly in front of the bar.
Take a full breath and brace, then initiate the movement by driving through the heels and extending the hips and knees explosively.

Keep the bar close to your body as you extend; as the bar passes the knees, perform an explosive shrug and pull with the elbows driving upward and outward to bring the bar toward chest/chin height (high pull).
Aim to lead with the elbows so the forearms and delts receive the pull; fully extend hips at the top and stand tall without hyperextending the lower back.
Lower the bar under control by reversing the motion: allow the hips to hinge, knees to bend, and guide the bar back to the floor while maintaining a neutral spine.
Reset stance and breathing between reps; inhale and brace before each lift and exhale at/after the top. Maintain knee tracking over toes and avoid rounding the back.
exercises.description: A compound, explosive lift performed from a wide (sumo) stance that combines a deadlift hip/leg drive with an upright high pull to bring the bar toward the chest. It trains lower-body extension and upper-body pulling (shoulder shrug and elbow lift) in a single fluid movement.

exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.

Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.

Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
